prerequisites: A Secoda workspace on a plan that exposes the API (Secoda Cloud or a self-hosted Secoda instance).
Access to your workspace's Workspace Settings so you can open the API section and generate a key.
An API key generated in Secoda; it grants the same access level as the user who created it, so create it from an account with the data access you need.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: Secoda API requests authenticate with the api_key field, sent as an HTTP Authorization header in the form 'Authorization: Bearer <Your API Key>'. The key is a token generated in your Secoda workspace and inherits the access level of the user who created it.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Secoda API key
setupSteps.body: In your Secoda workspace, open Workspace Settings and click API to view your API Keys list. Click Generate New API Key, then copy and store the key securely. It is shown once and carries the same access as the user who created it, so do not share it.

setupSteps.title: Provide the api_key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's required api_key field to the key you generated. pm sends it as 'Authorization: Bearer <api_key>' on every request to the Secoda API.

setupSteps.title: Confirm the correct regional API endpoint
setupSteps.body: Secoda exposes region-specific API base URLs that match your workspace URL: app.secoda.co uses api.secoda.co, eu.secoda.co uses eapi.secoda.co, and apac.secoda.co uses aapi.secoda.co. Self-hosted instances use your custom domain. Make sure the key is generated in, and used against, the same workspace/region.

sources.title: Secoda API - Getting started with Secoda
